Models

User

Description: Represents an HR Administrator or Recruiter using the TalentMatch AI platform.

Fields

Name Type Attributes Required Comment
id Int
  • @id
  • @default(autoincrement())
 Yes Unique identifier generated automatically by the database.
email String
  • @unique
 Yes The HR Admin's corporate email address. Used for authentication.
password String
  • -
Yes Hashed password for secure login. Never expose this to the frontend.
createdAt DateTime
  • @default(now())
 Yes Timestamp of when the HR Admin account was created.
updatedAt DateTime
  • @updatedAt
 Yes Timestamp of the last time the HR Admin account details were updated.
role UserRole
  • @default(USER)
 Yes -
candidates Candidate[]
  • -
Yes -
positions Position[]
  • -
Yes -

Operations

findUnique

Find zero or one User

// Get one User
const user = await prisma.user.findUnique({
  where: {
    // ... provide filter here
  }
})

Input

Name Type Required
where UserWhereUniqueInput Yes

Output

Type: User
Required: No
List: No

findFirst

Find first User

// Get one User
const user = await prisma.user.findFirst({
  where: {
    // ... provide filter here
  }
})

Input

Name Type Required
where UserWhereInput No
orderBy UserOrderByWithRelationInput[] | UserOrderByWithRelationInput No
cursor UserWhereUniqueInput No
take Int No
skip Int No
distinct UserScalarFieldEnum | UserScalarFieldEnum[] No

Output

Type: User
Required: No
List: No

findMany

Find zero or more User

// Get all User
const User = await prisma.user.findMany()
// Get first 10 User
const User = await prisma.user.findMany({ take: 10 })

Input

Name Type Required
where UserWhereInput No
orderBy UserOrderByWithRelationInput[] | UserOrderByWithRelationInput No
cursor UserWhereUniqueInput No
take Int No
skip Int No
distinct UserScalarFieldEnum | UserScalarFieldEnum[] No

Output

Type: User
Required: Yes
List: Yes

create

Create one User

// Create one User
const User = await prisma.user.create({
  data: {
    // ... data to create a User
  }
})

Input

Name Type Required
data UserCreateInput | UserUncheckedCreateInput Yes

Output

Type: User
Required: Yes
List: No

delete

Delete one User

// Delete one User
const User = await prisma.user.delete({
  where: {
    // ... filter to delete one User
  }
})

Input

Name Type Required
where UserWhereUniqueInput Yes

Output

Type: User
Required: No
List: No

update

Update one User

// Update one User
const user = await prisma.user.update({
  where: {
    // ... provide filter here
  },
  data: {
    // ... provide data here
  }
})

Input

Name Type Required
data UserUpdateInput | UserUncheckedUpdateInput Yes
where UserWhereUniqueInput Yes

Output

Type: User
Required: No
List: No

deleteMany

Delete zero or more User

// Delete a few User
const { count } = await prisma.user.deleteMany({
  where: {
    // ... provide filter here
  }
})

Input

Name Type Required
where UserWhereInput No
limit Int No

Output

Type: AffectedRowsOutput
Required: Yes
List: No

updateMany

Update zero or one User

const { count } = await prisma.user.updateMany({
  where: {
    // ... provide filter here
  },
  data: {
    // ... provide data here
  }
})

Input

Name Type Required
data UserUpdateManyMutationInput | UserUncheckedUpdateManyInput Yes
where UserWhereInput No
limit Int No

Output

Type: AffectedRowsOutput
Required: Yes
List: No

upsert

Create or update one User

// Update or create a User
const user = await prisma.user.upsert({
  create: {
    // ... data to create a User
  },
  update: {
    // ... in case it already exists, update
  },
  where: {
    // ... the filter for the User we want to update
  }
})

Input

Name Type Required
where UserWhereUniqueInput Yes
create UserCreateInput | UserUncheckedCreateInput Yes
update UserUpdateInput | UserUncheckedUpdateInput Yes

Output

Type: User
Required: Yes
List: No

Position

Description: Defines the specific job requirements created by an HR Admin. This model acts as the baseline criteria for the AI to evaluate Candidates against.

Fields

Name Type Attributes Required Comment
id Int
  • @id
  • @default(autoincrement())
 Yes Unique identifier generated automatically by the database.
role String
  • -
Yes The official title of the job opening (e.g., "Senior Backend Developer").
yearsOfExperience Int
  • -
Yes The strict minimum years of professional experience required.
technicalSkills Json
  • -
Yes Required hard skills for the role (e.g., "Node.js, Express, MySQL, Prisma").
optionalTechnicalSkills Json
  • -
Yes "Nice-to-have" skills that boost a candidate's match score but are not mandatory.
softSkills Json
  • -
Yes Required interpersonal skills (e.g., "Leadership, Clear Communication").
description String
  • -
Yes A detailed summary of the day-to-day responsibilities and expectations.
education String
  • -
Yes Minimum academic background required (e.g., "Systems Engineering Degree").
languages Json
  • -
Yes Spoken languages required for the role (e.g., "English (B2), Spanish (Native)").
userId Int
  • -
Yes Foreign key linking this position to the HR Admin who created it.
createdAt DateTime
  • @default(now())
 Yes Timestamp of when this job requirement was drafted.
updatedAt DateTime
  • @updatedAt
 Yes Timestamp of the last time HR modified these requirements.
candidates Candidate[]
  • -
Yes -
user User
  • -
Yes -
vacancies Vacancy[]
  • -
Yes -

Operations

findUnique

Find zero or one Position

// Get one Position
const position = await prisma.position.findUnique({
  where: {
    // ... provide filter here
  }
})

Input

Name Type Required
where PositionWhereUniqueInput Yes

Output

Type: Position
Required: No
List: No

findFirst

Find first Position

// Get one Position
const position = await prisma.position.findFirst({
  where: {
    // ... provide filter here
  }
})

Input

Name Type Required
where PositionWhereInput No
orderBy PositionOrderByWithRelationInput[] | PositionOrderByWithRelationInput No
cursor PositionWhereUniqueInput No
take Int No
skip Int No
distinct PositionScalarFieldEnum | PositionScalarFieldEnum[] No

Output

Type: Position
Required: No
List: No

findMany

Find zero or more Position

// Get all Position
const Position = await prisma.position.findMany()
// Get first 10 Position
const Position = await prisma.position.findMany({ take: 10 })

Input

Name Type Required
where PositionWhereInput No
orderBy PositionOrderByWithRelationInput[] | PositionOrderByWithRelationInput No
cursor PositionWhereUniqueInput No
take Int No
skip Int No
distinct PositionScalarFieldEnum | PositionScalarFieldEnum[] No

Output

Type: Position
Required: Yes
List: Yes

create

Create one Position

// Create one Position
const Position = await prisma.position.create({
  data: {
    // ... data to create a Position
  }
})

Input

Name Type Required
data PositionCreateInput | PositionUncheckedCreateInput Yes

Output

Type: Position
Required: Yes
List: No

delete

Delete one Position

// Delete one Position
const Position = await prisma.position.delete({
  where: {
    // ... filter to delete one Position
  }
})

Input

Name Type Required
where PositionWhereUniqueInput Yes

Output

Type: Position
Required: No
List: No

update

Update one Position

// Update one Position
const position = await prisma.position.update({
  where: {
    // ... provide filter here
  },
  data: {
    // ... provide data here
  }
})

Input

Name Type Required
data PositionUpdateInput | PositionUncheckedUpdateInput Yes
where PositionWhereUniqueInput Yes

Output

Type: Position
Required: No
List: No

deleteMany

Delete zero or more Position

// Delete a few Position
const { count } = await prisma.position.deleteMany({
  where: {
    // ... provide filter here
  }
})

Input

Name Type Required
where PositionWhereInput No
limit Int No

Output

Type: AffectedRowsOutput
Required: Yes
List: No

updateMany

Update zero or one Position

const { count } = await prisma.position.updateMany({
  where: {
    // ... provide filter here
  },
  data: {
    // ... provide data here
  }
})

Input

Name Type Required
data PositionUpdateManyMutationInput | PositionUncheckedUpdateManyInput Yes
where PositionWhereInput No
limit Int No

Output

Type: AffectedRowsOutput
Required: Yes
List: No

upsert

Create or update one Position

// Update or create a Position
const position = await prisma.position.upsert({
  create: {
    // ... data to create a Position
  },
  update: {
    // ... in case it already exists, update
  },
  where: {
    // ... the filter for the Position we want to update
  }
})

Input

Name Type Required
where PositionWhereUniqueInput Yes
create PositionCreateInput | PositionUncheckedCreateInput Yes
update PositionUpdateInput | PositionUncheckedUpdateInput Yes

Output

Type: Position
Required: Yes
List: No

Candidate

Description: Stores the AI-parsed information extracted from the candidate's uploaded CV. The raw data is saved in a JSON field, and specific metrics are mapped to individual columns for fast querying.

Fields

Name Type Attributes Required Comment
id Int
  • @id
  • @default(autoincrement())
 Yes Unique identifier generated automatically by the database.
fullName String
  • -
Yes The full name of the candidate, extracted by the AI.
email String
  • -
Yes The contact email of the candidate.
createdAt DateTime
  • @default(now())
 Yes Timestamp of when the CV was uploaded and parsed.
description String
  • -
Yes A brief AI-generated summary of the candidate's professional profile.
education String
  • -
Yes The candidate's highest level of formal education.
fileUrl String?
  • -
No The cloud hosting URL (e.g., Cloudinary) where the original PDF is stored.
languages Json
  • -
Yes Spoken languages identified in the CV.
optionalTechnicalSkills Json
  • -
Yes Additional or secondary technical skills found in the CV.
positionId Int?
  • -
No Optional foreign key. Links the candidate to a specific Position if uploaded directly for a role. If null, the candidate exists independently within the Global Talent Pool.
role String
  • -
Yes The primary job role the candidate is applying for or qualified for.
softSkills Json
  • -
Yes Interpersonal and communication skills identified by the AI.
technicalSkills Json
  • -
Yes Core technical skills extracted from the CV.
updatedAt DateTime
  • @updatedAt
 Yes Timestamp of the last update to the candidate's profile.
userId Int
  • -
Yes Foreign key linking this candidate to the HR Admin who uploaded the CV.
yearsOfExperience Int
  • -
Yes Total years of professional experience, parsed as an integer for mathematical comparison.
hash String
  • @unique
 Yes A unique SHA-256 hash of the parsed CV text. Used as a caching mechanism to prevent duplicate OpenAI API calls.
rawApiPayload Json
  • -
Yes The complete, raw JSON payload returned by the AI for backup and auditing.
position Position?
  • -
No -
user User
  • -
Yes -
matchResults MatchResult[]
  • -
Yes -

Operations

findUnique

Find zero or one Candidate

// Get one Candidate
const candidate = await prisma.candidate.findUnique({
  where: {
    // ... provide filter here
  }
})

Input

Name Type Required
where CandidateWhereUniqueInput Yes

Output

Type: Candidate
Required: No
List: No

findFirst

Find first Candidate

// Get one Candidate
const candidate = await prisma.candidate.findFirst({
  where: {
    // ... provide filter here
  }
})

Input

Name Type Required
where CandidateWhereInput No
orderBy CandidateOrderByWithRelationInput[] | CandidateOrderByWithRelationInput No
cursor CandidateWhereUniqueInput No
take Int No
skip Int No
distinct CandidateScalarFieldEnum | CandidateScalarFieldEnum[] No

Output

Type: Candidate
Required: No
List: No

findMany

Find zero or more Candidate

// Get all Candidate
const Candidate = await prisma.candidate.findMany()
// Get first 10 Candidate
const Candidate = await prisma.candidate.findMany({ take: 10 })

Input

Name Type Required
where CandidateWhereInput No
orderBy CandidateOrderByWithRelationInput[] | CandidateOrderByWithRelationInput No
cursor CandidateWhereUniqueInput No
take Int No
skip Int No
distinct CandidateScalarFieldEnum | CandidateScalarFieldEnum[] No

Output

Type: Candidate
Required: Yes
List: Yes

create

Create one Candidate

// Create one Candidate
const Candidate = await prisma.candidate.create({
  data: {
    // ... data to create a Candidate
  }
})

Input

Name Type Required
data CandidateCreateInput | CandidateUncheckedCreateInput Yes

Output

Type: Candidate
Required: Yes
List: No

delete

Delete one Candidate

// Delete one Candidate
const Candidate = await prisma.candidate.delete({
  where: {
    // ... filter to delete one Candidate
  }
})

Input

Name Type Required
where CandidateWhereUniqueInput Yes

Output

Type: Candidate
Required: No
List: No

update

Update one Candidate

// Update one Candidate
const candidate = await prisma.candidate.update({
  where: {
    // ... provide filter here
  },
  data: {
    // ... provide data here
  }
})

Input

Name Type Required
data CandidateUpdateInput | CandidateUncheckedUpdateInput Yes
where CandidateWhereUniqueInput Yes

Output

Type: Candidate
Required: No
List: No

deleteMany

Delete zero or more Candidate

// Delete a few Candidate
const { count } = await prisma.candidate.deleteMany({
  where: {
    // ... provide filter here
  }
})

Input

Name Type Required
where CandidateWhereInput No
limit Int No

Output

Type: AffectedRowsOutput
Required: Yes
List: No

updateMany

Update zero or one Candidate

const { count } = await prisma.candidate.updateMany({
  where: {
    // ... provide filter here
  },
  data: {
    // ... provide data here
  }
})

Input

Name Type Required
data CandidateUpdateManyMutationInput | CandidateUncheckedUpdateManyInput Yes
where CandidateWhereInput No
limit Int No

Output

Type: AffectedRowsOutput
Required: Yes
List: No

upsert

Create or update one Candidate

// Update or create a Candidate
const candidate = await prisma.candidate.upsert({
  create: {
    // ... data to create a Candidate
  },
  update: {
    // ... in case it already exists, update
  },
  where: {
    // ... the filter for the Candidate we want to update
  }
})

Input

Name Type Required
where CandidateWhereUniqueInput Yes
create CandidateCreateInput | CandidateUncheckedCreateInput Yes
update CandidateUpdateInput | CandidateUncheckedUpdateInput Yes

Output

Type: Candidate
Required: Yes
List: No

Vacancy

Description: Represents an active, publicly or internally open job posting. It uses the baseline requirements from the Position model to accept candidates.

Fields

Name Type Attributes Required Comment
id Int
  • @id
  • @default(autoincrement())
 Yes Unique identifier generated automatically by the database.
title String
  • -
Yes The public-facing title of the active job posting.
openDate DateTime
  • -
Yes The date the job posting officially opened for applications.
closeDate DateTime
  • -
Yes The deadline or closing date for the job posting.
positionId Int
  • -
Yes Foreign key linking this active posting to its baseline requirements (Position).
createdAt DateTime
  • @default(now())
 Yes Timestamp of when the vacancy was created in the system.
updatedAt DateTime
  • @updatedAt
 Yes Timestamp of the last update to the vacancy details.
status VacancyStatus
  • @default(OPEN)
 Yes The current operational state of the vacancy.
matchResults MatchResult[]
  • -
Yes -
position Position
  • -
Yes -
userId Int
  • -
Yes Foreign key linking vacancy directly to the HR Admin owner. Enables fast dashboard queries without JOINs through Position.

Operations

findUnique

Find zero or one Vacancy

// Get one Vacancy
const vacancy = await prisma.vacancy.findUnique({
  where: {
    // ... provide filter here
  }
})

Input

Name Type Required
where VacancyWhereUniqueInput Yes

Output

Type: Vacancy
Required: No
List: No

findFirst

Find first Vacancy

// Get one Vacancy
const vacancy = await prisma.vacancy.findFirst({
  where: {
    // ... provide filter here
  }
})

Input

Name Type Required
where VacancyWhereInput No
orderBy VacancyOrderByWithRelationInput[] | VacancyOrderByWithRelationInput No
cursor VacancyWhereUniqueInput No
take Int No
skip Int No
distinct VacancyScalarFieldEnum | VacancyScalarFieldEnum[] No

Output

Type: Vacancy
Required: No
List: No

// Get first 10 Vacancy
const Vacancy = await prisma.vacancy.findMany({ take: 10 })

Name Type Required

  },
  update: {

update VacancyUpdateInput | VacancyUncheckedUpdateInput Yes

Output

Type: Vacancy
Required: Yes
List: No

MatchResult

Description: The junction table storing the AI's final evaluation score. Connects a specific Candidate to a specific Vacancy.
Name Value
@@unique
  • candidateId
  • vacancyId
@@index
  • candidateId
  • vacancyId

Fields

Name Type Attributes Required Comment
id Int
  • @id
  • @default(autoincrement())
 Yes Unique identifier generated automatically by the database.
matchScore Int
  • -
Yes The final compatibility percentage (0-100) calculated by the AI.
candidateId Int
  • -
Yes Foreign key linking to the evaluated candidate.
vacancyId Int
  • -
Yes Foreign key linking to the specific job posting they were evaluated for.
createdAt DateTime
  • @default(now())
 Yes Timestamp of when the AI completed the evaluation and generated the score.
educationScore Int
  • -
Yes Score (0-10) assessing if the candidate's educational background meets the job's criteria.
experienceScore Int
  • -
Yes Score (0-20) evaluating the candidate's years of experience against the required baseline.
hardSkillsScore Int
  • -
Yes Score (0-30) reflecting how well the candidate's technical skills match the position requirements.
languagesScore Int
  • -
Yes Score (0-15) indicating proficiency in the required and optional languages.
normalizedCandidate Json
  • -
Yes A cached JSON snapshot of the candidate's parsed profile at the exact moment of evaluation. Storing this prevents redundant LLM API calls and guarantees the frontend UI can display the exact data the scoring engine used, ensuring auditability and deterministic rendering.
redFlags String?
  • -
No A concise alert highlighting critical missing requirements or experience gaps detected by the AI. Optional if no flags are found.
roleScore Int
  • -
Yes Score (0-15) based on the relevance of the candidate's past job titles to the current vacancy.
softSkillsScore Int
  • -
Yes Score (0-10) evaluating the presence of required interpersonal and professional skills.
summary String
  • -
Yes A brief, AI-generated text summary justifying the overall match score based on the candidate's profile and the position requirements.
candidate Candidate
  • -
Yes -
vacancy Vacancy
  • -
Yes -

Operations

findUnique

Find zero or one MatchResult

// Get one MatchResult
const matchResult = await prisma.matchResult.findUnique({
  where: {
    // ... provide filter here
  }
})

Input

Name Type Required
where MatchResultWhereUniqueInput Yes

Output

Type: MatchResult
Required: No
List: No

Find first MatchResult

  where: {
    // ... provide filter here
  }
})

UserWhereInput

Name Type Nullable
AND UserWhereInput | UserWhereInput[] No
OR UserWhereInput[] No
NOT UserWhereInput | UserWhereInput[] No

updatedAt DateTimeFilter | DateTime No
role EnumUserRoleFilter | UserRole No

positions PositionListRelationFilter No

Name Type Nullable
